dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used Donchian Channel (dc_upper, dc_lower) to represent the n-period channel breakout mechanism. dc_upper approximates the 'maximum close over past n periods' and dc_lower the minimum. The 10-period lookback is implicit in Donchian Channel calculation. Entry requires break above upper band AND confirmation via sma_5. Exit triggers on close below lower band OR after 8 bars. Direction is LONG based on 'enter long position' statement. Target regime includes TRENDING_UP (primary trend-following context) and ANY to allow flexibility.

backtest_rule.reason: Used Donchian Channel (dc_upper, dc_lower) to represent the n-period channel breakout mechanism. dc_upper approximates the 'maximum close over past n periods' and dc_lower the minimum. The 10-period lookback is implicit in Donchian Channel calculation. Entry requires break above upper band AND confirmation via sma_5. Exit triggers on close below lower band OR after 8 bars. Direction is LONG based on 'enter long position' statement. Target regime includes TRENDING_UP (primary trend-following context) and ANY to allow flexibility.

source_rule_text: Enter a long position when price breaks above the maximum value of a lookback channel; the simplicity and effectiveness of channel breakouts makes them viable as a core trend-following mechanism.
